accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and accurate context (MATE Act, Section 303 CSA, December 29, 2022 enactment of original requirement) is verified.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the substance use disorder/prescribing context and organization names are all supported by the bill or accurate real-world context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill clarifies and expands training requirements for prescribers of controlled substances under the CSA.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies amendments to Section 303 of the Controlled Substances Act, including the subsection redesignation.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill contains no material exceptions, eligibility limits, or carve-outs to summarize.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the retroactive effective date of December 29, 2022.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures all operative effects: redesignation, added organizations in both subparagraphs, podiatric medicine pathways, schools of pharmacy, and retroactivity.

objective_tone.why: Neutral, descriptive language with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, logically structured, and internally consistent.

concise.why: Efficient with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: All named organizations (AAFP, APMA, AGD, AOA, Council on Podiatric Medical Education, APhA, ACPE, APNA, AAN) and the statute match the bill text.

correct_figures.why: The only figure, the December 29, 2022 effective date, is stated correctly.
